Q1: I think it can still support students in other Arab countries, but it would be more reliable in Qatar since essay prompts and curricula differ between countries.
Q2: There local curriculum bodies or teacher communities in each target country but I'm not sure how much their scoring conventions differ from Qatar's, so it is something that would need to be further researched, but generally speaking Modern Standard Arabic is meant to be standard across all Arab countries.
Q3: It would be more helpful for the system to produce actionable revision suggestions and provide natural-language explanations of why students lost points.
Q4: The benchmark is limited to 8 prompts that are either explanatory or persuasive so it is not extensive for a wide range of essay types like literary analysis, religious texts, or cultural commentary, which could be assigned to students to write.
